ncert_truth_statements: Electric current is the rate of flow of electric charge through a cross-section of a conductor: I = Q/t
Conventional current flows from the positive terminal to the negative terminal of a cell through the external circuit
Electrons flow from the negative terminal to the positive terminal, opposite to the direction of conventional current
The SI unit of electric current is the ampere; one ampere equals one coulomb of charge flowing per second
In a metallic conductor, current is due to the drift of free electrons under the influence of an electric field
